flagship_module.claim: Hypothesis: a prenylation and small-GTPase-trafficking module anchored on PGGT1B modulates stimulated human CD4+ T-cell activation.

evidence_ladder.detail: PGGT1B has 3,014 stimulated DE genes and CCDC22 has 619 in the frozen frontier.
PGGT1B and CCDC22 are hits in Shifrut 2018 screen 1107.
All four members are non-hits in Schmidt 2022 screen 2427.
STRING neighborhoods connect PGGT1B to prenylation and small-GTPase partners and the other members to trafficking complexes.
All four members have DICE activated CD4 expression rows.

flagship_module.mechanism: A proposal that stimulated CD4+ activation depends on prenylation-linked small-GTPase signaling and endosomal trafficking logistics.

member_evidence.why_not_accepted: The evidence supports a hypothesis, but it is computation over released data, not wet-lab or clinical truth.
The evidence supports a hypothesis, but it is computation over released data, not wet-lab or clinical truth.
The evidence supports a hypothesis, but it is computation over released data, not wet-lab or clinical truth.
The evidence supports a hypothesis, but it is computation over released data, not wet-lab or clinical truth.

flagship_module.name: Prenylation and trafficking control of activation

refutation_experiment.readout: CRISPRi followed by activation-marker flow cytometry and targeted RNA-seq at 8h and 48h
refutation_experiment.refutes_if: orthogonal knockdown produces no reproducible activation-program shift for the module, or the effect is equally strong at Rest and in non-immune controls
refutation_experiment.system: stimulated primary human CD4+ T cells

flagship_module.why_not_accepted: This is not wet-lab or clinical truth. It is a proposal supported by released-data computation and external context, with a direct refutation experiment.

honest_ceiling: computation over released data, not wet-lab or clinical truth
